opp.ais_why:
• The 89-minute 12/9/2025 Patty Rice exec demo generated 11 action items. None mention AI, Merlin, Agentic, Anna, or Intake. Ritika Ritolia's commitments cover OCR extraction of late-charge line items from supplier invoices, supplier self-registration via a public web link routed through an approval queue, payment gateway integration, and approve/reject buttons inside email notifications: operational AP and eProcurement plumbing, none AI-led.
• Buyer Patty Rice's single attributed item is receiving a follow-up Excel. Steve Ovadje is committed to a detailed pricing proposal and the follow-up calendar invite. The wider discovery record frames the drivers as PR-to-PO workflow, punch-out support, GL coding automation, invoice routing, and duplicate detection: operationally replacing the sunsetting Procurement Partners system.
• AI is flagged Very Critical at the account level, but no buyer-stated AI requirement, AI demo ask, or AI POC has surfaced across seven opportunity-level Avoma calls. Transcript keyword review confirmed AI signal absent in buyer attribution. Until the account-level AI intent shows up inside this deal, the score holds in AI Resistant and confidence moves to High.

sources: Avoma follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026 (Zycus SC, speaker_id 5)
metrics.narrative: The business problem is clear and quantified at the operational level: Patty Rice (5 Nov 2025) said current spend is '$100 per community per month... about 32 communities' (~$38.4k/year), and Zycus quoted $80k-$150k/year, a 2-4x increase. On 17 Apr 2026 the Zycus solutions consultant framed the value as raising spend visibility 'from 80% to 100%' across 28 communities and cutting AP exception work in half (AP team of three spends 24-48 hours resolving exceptions). Patty gave a vivid savings anecdote on 13 Nov 2025: a $90k kitchen-equipment bid where her boss's AI found another $15k of savings ('how much have I not saved in the past?'). What is missing is a finance-owned ROI case that justifies the premium over the ~$38.4k incumbent and the cheaper SmartPO+Ottimate split; without that the price gap remains the central objection.

champion.narrative: Patty Rice (Purchasing) is a developing champion, not a strong one. Evidence of advocacy: she revived a closed-lost deal to revisit Zycus after 'lackluster competition' (Next Step 13 May), pulled her executive team into the 17 Apr demo, and said 18 Apr 'I am glad to be able to share your value proposition with our larger group' and requested the recording to share with an absent stakeholder. What is missing: she does not own budget ('I get to decide the front end purchasing piece' but not invoicing or final spend), AP-team and CFO sign-off sit outside her authority, and critically her own lean has shifted - on 3 Jun she told the relevant stakeholder is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now.' A champion now leaning to a competitor is at risk. There is no second strong internal advocate; Justin Witkowski (VP IT) and John Hanna (CFO) have not been activated as Zycus supporters.

competition.narrative: This is a competitive deal Zycus is currently losing the internal lean on. As of 3 Jun 2026 Patty stated Kisco is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now,' with Zycus only a fallback 'if we run into any non-starters' - making Coupa the strongest current threat. Zip entered as a serious contender (Next Step 13 May: 'Now we are up against Zip') and Steve's 29 Apr finance summary explicitly compared 'Zip, Coupa and Smart PO + Ottimate.' The cheaper split of SmartPO/SmartProcure/Medprocure (purchasing) + Ottimate/Automate (invoicing) remains attractive on price (~$40k-$60k vs Zycus $80k-$150k) and Patty rated Ottimate's 100% AI invoice reading above Zycus's OCR-based approach (11 Feb). GEP was dropped from the evaluation (11 Feb). The incumbent Procurement Partners is being displaced (forced Oracle On-Care migration Sept-Oct 2026). How Zycus wins: prove price-to-value with a finance-owned ROI case, neutralise the Ottimate AI-invoicing perception with a head-to-head AP Smartdesk accuracy demo to the AP team, and use the end-of-quarter Vendor-of-Choice discount as a forcing lever.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is concrete and buyer-articulated. The forcing pain: Procurement Partners is sunsetting in ~6 months (stated 5 Nov 2025) with forced migration to the relevant stakeholder, which Patty rejects ('I don't care for it... they're not force migrating me anywhere'). Operational pain: ~20-24 punch-out vendors per community with inconsistent coverage; cumbersome requisition setup and weak compliance enforcement ('they just go buy it'); only ~80% spend visibility; duplicate invoices causing overpayments ('we've ended up overpaying vendors'); a lean AP team of three spending 24-48 hours on exceptions; and Patty operating as 'a team of one' unable to escape tactical tailspin negotiations. Pain owner is Patty Rice (purchasing) for the front end and the AP/finance team for invoicing. The pain is real but mitigated by a cheaper incumbent-replacement path (SmartPO+Ottimate) that addresses the must-change without the Zycus premium.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are surfaced but unresolved. On 11 Feb 2026 Patty learned Zycus requires a 5-year minimum term ('Kisco does not like 5 year agreements'); Steve confirmed a 3-year option exists but with smaller discounts, and that billing is annual only (monthly negotiable). User licensing is 450 users in scope with 50-user eProcurement bundles at $3,500 each. IT involvement (NetSuite and Avid integration) is expected and VP IT Justin Witkowski has been looped in (29 Apr email). The Procurement Partners sunset / forced Oracle On-Care migration scheduled Sept-Oct 2026 is the real forcing function for paper - a decision must land well before then to allow implementation lead time. No MSA, SOW, legal review or InfoSec review has started.

sources: Avoma 17 Apr 2026 participants (John Hanna, Chrissie Ripa)
Email 29 Apr 2026 to John Hanna and Chrissie Ripa
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but passive. CFO John Hanna and VP of Finance/Accounting Chrissie Ripa were invited to and attended the 17 Apr 2026 executive demo (Next Step 4/3: 'Demo Scheduled with Patty, CFO (John Hanna) and Chrissie (VP of Accounting) for 4/17'; 17 Apr Avoma participants list confirms Hanna and Ripa). Steve sent a finance-focused executive summary to John and Chrissie on 29 Apr and shared it again 4 May ('Shared Finance Focused Exec summary'). However, there is no evidence either drove urgency, defended Zycus competitively, or committed budget. Budget approval has been pending since Patty's 13 Nov 2025 'meeting with VP of finance later today' and remained unconfirmed through Feb and into June. The next move is to convert the finance summary into a quantified ROI conversation that gives Hanna a reason to choose Zycus over the cheaper alternatives.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a multi-vendor bake-off run by Patty (purchasing) with finance and IT as approvers. Read from the call sequence: intro (5 Nov) and discovery (13 Nov) with Patty alone; product demos (25 Nov, 9 Dec) deepening into pricing; a Feb catch-up confirming an active project targeting an April decision; then an executive demo on 17 Apr that pulled in CFO John Hanna, VP Finance Chrissie Ripa and VP IT Justin Witkowski - a genuine escalation in seniority. After 17 Apr the Kisco team said it would 'debrief internally after your other demos over the next 2-3 weeks.' Patty confirmed (6 May email) 'we have only had one other demo with two more to come,' and Next Step 13 May noted '2 additional demos and provide a decision by 6/22.' AP team buy-in is a required gate for the invoicing module. The committed 22 Jun decision passed with no material update, and on 3 Jun Patty signalled the internal lean is now toward Coupa.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented across the call series. Patty Rice's stated must-haves: ease of use for non-tech-savvy frontline staff across 28-32 communities (5 Nov 2025), full punch-out support (~20-24 vendors per community, Grainger/HD Supply/Amazon Business), GL coding configurable per category/vendor with optional approval workflows, duplicate-invoice detection and near-100% invoice capture, and line-item-level reporting (25 Nov 2025). On AP/invoicing she explicitly weighted invoice-reading accuracy, praising competitor Ottimate/Automate's '100% AI' reading as 'a much higher level of accuracy than OCR' (11 Feb 2026) - a criterion where Zycus's AP Smartdesk did not clearly win. Price-to-value versus the ~$38.4k incumbent is the dominant decision criterion, which is why the cheaper SmartPO+Ottimate split and Coupa remain live.

Recommend: (1) pricing hold expires 15 Jul (3 weeks out) — communicate now, (2) offer executive workshop (procurement best practices, AI adoption roadmap for senior living) to create value beyond the demo cycle and pull John Hanna + Chrissy Ripa back into conversation, or (3) propose a mutual close plan (decision by 10 Jul, contracting by 31 Jul, go-live by 30 Sept ahead of Procurement Partners sunset).

competitors.name: Procurement Partners (incumbent, forced to Oracle On-Care)
competitors.quote: they are gonna be sunsetting the platform ... force migrate all of their existing customers over ... and I don't care for it

competitors.name: SmartPO / Smartprocure / Medprocure
competitors.quote: Smartprocure or Smartpo or Medprocure... pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today
competitors.how_we_win: SmartPO is a front-end purchasing-only tool with limited functionality (no sourcing, no integrated invoicing, no end-to-end reporting). Position Zycus as the only platform that delivers full procurement visibility, integrated P2P, and AI-driven efficiency across purchasing AND invoicing. Make the split-solution path (SmartPO + Automate) unacceptable by emphasizing integration risk, dual-vendor management overhead, and lack of unified data model.

competitors.name: Automate
competitors.quote: automate is a 100% AI ... it reads with a much higher level of accuracy than OCR

competitors.name: Procurement Partners / Oracle On-Care
competitors.quote: they are gonna be sunsetting the platform... force migrate all of their existing customers over to... Oncare, and I've seen it... I don't care for it.
competitors.how_we_win: Use the Sept-Oct 2026 forced-migration deadline as the compelling event that forces a decision before then.

competitors.name: SmartPO / SmartProcure / Medprocure (purchasing) + Automate (invoicing)
competitors.quote: the pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today... automate is a 100% AI... reads with a much higher level of accuracy than OCR
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe on total cost and risk of stitching two vendors vs one AI-native suite; match Automate's AI-accuracy claim head-on with AP Smartdesk demo to the AP team.

competitors.name: Procurement Partners / Oracle On-Care (incumbent)
competitors.quote: they're planning to force migrate... over to... Oncare... I don't care for it
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent being displaced by the sunset; reinforce the Sept-Oct migration deadline as the forcing event.

competitors.name: SmartPO / SmartProcure / Medprocure + Ottimate (Automate)
competitors.quote: the pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today... automate is a 100% AI... reads with a much higher level of accuracy than OCR.
competitors.how_we_win: Run a head-to-head AP Smartdesk accuracy demo to the AP team to neutralise the Ottimate '100% AI' perception; show one integrated platform avoids the two-vendor split and integration burden.

hard.ais_why:
• The 89-minute 12/9/2025 Patty Rice exec demo generated 11 action items. None mention AI, Merlin, Agentic, Anna, or Intake. Ritika Ritolia's commitments cover OCR extraction of late-charge line items from supplier invoices, supplier self-registration via a public web link routed through an approval queue, payment gateway integration, and approve/reject buttons inside email notifications: operational AP and eProcurement plumbing, none AI-led.
• Buyer Patty Rice's single attributed item is receiving a follow-up Excel. Steve Ovadje is committed to a detailed pricing proposal and the follow-up calendar invite. The wider discovery record frames the drivers as PR-to-PO workflow, punch-out support, GL coding automation, invoice routing, and duplicate detection: operationally replacing the sunsetting Procurement Partners system.
• AI is flagged Very Critical at the account level, but no buyer-stated AI requirement, AI demo ask, or AI POC has surfaced across seven opportunity-level Avoma calls. Transcript keyword review confirmed AI signal absent in buyer attribution. Until the account-level AI intent shows up inside this deal, the score holds in AI Resistant and confidence moves to High.

Position Zycus as the only platform that delivers full procurement visibility, integrated P2P, and AI-driven efficiency across purchasing AND invoicing. value.name: Automate
value.quote: Automate... AI actually reads the invoice instead of running it through OCR... 100% AI... much higher level of... Accuracy than OCR
value.how_we_win: Automate is an invoicing-only point solution with no procurement functionality. Patty perceives it as superior on AI accuracy vs Zycus, which is a messaging gap—Zycus AP Smartdesk uses AI extraction (not OCR) and was demonstrated 9 Dec 2025, but the message did not land. Close this gap: re-emphasize AP Smartdesk AI extraction, fraud detection, and duplicate checking, and position Automate as a siloed invoicing tool that cannot deliver end-to-end procurement control or integrated reporting. Deliver side-by-side AI accuracy comparison if possible.
